action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 12's run with FocalLoss collapsed: val AP peaked at 0.0275 and training loss flatlined at 0.0087, a massive regression from the 0.2087 test AP in iteration 10. Analysis: FocalLoss (gamma=2, alpha=0.75) is the culprit—it down-weights easy negatives so aggressively that the model learns to predict all negatives, yielding artificially low loss and near-zero AP. The architectural upgrades (JK, wider network, virtual node) are sound but were undermined by the broken loss. Decision: Revert to standard BCE. Keep the larger architecture (768-dim, 8 layers, JK, virtual node, mean+max pool) to push beyond the previous 0.20 baseline. Use batch size 1024 to cut wall-clock time (~30-40 min for 30 epochs). Train with AdamW, cosine LR with warmup, gradient clipping, and save the best val-AP checkpoint.
